item.title: Re: Staff Post- Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded: Super belated, but congratulations!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It even is in my state, close to where I live.

Wow! I hope there are some parks nearby so the article is believable! We tried to use a location that would work for both the legends and the real world landmarks.

Und die Mondgnome sind wirklich süß. <3

I am happy you think so! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A German SCP! I love it!
It even is in my state, close to where I live.

Und die Mondgnome sind wirklich süß. <3

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded: I had vanished into nothingness to work on a million music projects and get married but then I remembered this and just logged on to see this over a month later. Totally cool with this.

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded: Will do!

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
Hmm. I like the link, not so much the way the link is added (since it seems to imply that 2957 may be variations of 1054, and they're not). Maybe a footnote noting that these are similar in appearance to SCP-1054 instances?

That said, I'll defer to coffeepunk here.

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded: I'm in favor, but let's also see what coffeepunk and Zyn say^^

item.title: Staff Post- Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
edit: edit made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show
- hide

To link to another skip about garden gnomes, I propose the following edit:

The object's appearance bears strong resemblance to antique German "Night Watchman" garden gnome figurines

to

The object's appearance bears strong resemblance to antique German "Night Watchman" garden gnome figurines

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Tag Reversion (Open)
item.content:encoded: Sorry about that, I was reworking the review-attention-levels on the German branch where we are using mutliple o's to indicate the level, and must have added the tag to the original instead of the translation…

item.title: Staff Post - Tag Reversion (Open)
item.content:encoded: Noting that the recently-added tag 'oo' has been reverted, since it does not correspond to any valid tags used by the site.

item.title: !!! German Moon Gnomes!
item.content:encoded:
How did I miss this???

(Belated) Big thanks to our friends at the German branch! Happy Holidays! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2957 is now translated into German.

It also features the original interview which was translated into English for the EN-version.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Enthusiastic +1 for the gnomes and their little blue bobble hats

(ﾉ◕ヮ◕)ﾉ*:・ﾟ✧*:・ﾟ✧*:・ﾟ✧

MOND GNOME MOND GNOME MOND GNOME

Please can we get an edit to change "seeweed" to "seaweed" in the first trade log?

I asked SpectralDragon in chat to get it, since I'm currently at a library with kinda weirdly-restrictive wifi that doesn't always process edits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know just enough German folklore for Zyn's last comment to havve drawn me in, and was definitely not disappointed! I like the way these Mond Gnomen are of a more friendly persuasion, and we only get hints that there is some kind of rebel faction which are much less pleasant.

For comparison, take a look at the Boggart, which is my local version and apparently never nice.

Enthusiastic +1 for the gnomes and their little blue bobble hats

MOND GNOME MOND GNOME MOND GNOME

ETA: Please can we get an edit to change "seeweed" to "seaweed" in the first trade log?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr_Grom is to thank for the German mythology present in this!

Here are two links that were referenced during the writing:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kobold
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heinzelm%C3%A4nnchen

The second one explains why this SCP object was not found in Cologne. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reading it, I couldn't tell if these were an actual old myth or not, so I'd say you nailed it. +1 and a saucer of milk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Zyn is to thank for that! She pulled it all together and made it sound good. I basically just chucked ideas at our drafts.

EDIT: Whoops, that was meant as a response to Shaggy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: MONDGNOME MONDGNOME MONDGNOME

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We spent a lot of time hashing out ideas in the sandbox before we started writing the draft piece-by-piece. It's been quite the journey to get the work to where it is now! :D

I'm glad you liked this. Thanks for reading!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Including "bad moon gnomes" didn't make me understand the article any better, and just felt like an attempt to introduce some sorely unneeded pathos/menace into an already-great article.

Incidentally, we had a lot less kobolde/dark moon gnomes material in the early draft stages. Several of the reviewers said they hoped for a bit more on the baddie betrayers, hence the addition of the second addendum.

I'm glad you liked the article, though! That table was a headache and a half to compile, lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like night and day! I remember the previous version of this. There's been a remarkable overhaul in not just the tone; but the story, the characters, the trades…

A lack of cohesion plagued the last iteration, this is quite the opposite. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Understandable though I think it's fair to mention that historically Koboldes are opposite of the "good" gnomes our little moon buddies share some concepts with. So there is a real basis for it other than just being spooky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually like this quite a bit. As a dutchman might say "Elves - they're just so silly".

Up until the interview, this was pretty much perfect. However, I feel that the interview was an attempt to tack on narrative that was both superflous and underdeveloped. Conceptually, "moon gnomes" is pretty solid. Including "bad moon gnomes" didn't make me understand the article any better, and just felt like an attempt to introduce some sorely unneeded pathos/menace into an already-great article.

Still, solid +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: MOON GNOMES MOON GNOMES MOON GNOMES

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: MOON GNOMES MOON GNOMES MOON GNOMES

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: MOON GNOMES MOON GNOMES MOON GNOMES

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it sort of is German-ish- you see a lot Germanglish (ala Spanglish) words like that, especially in the Midwest and South: Beergarden, icehouse (icebox sort of came from that one), bum (as in hobo), grubhouse, uproar…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, „hochtrabend” has so many possible translations, I just guessed one ;)

item.title: Re: MOOOOOON GNOOOOOOMESS
item.content:encoded: Thanks Conwell! I really appreciate your reading over the piece during its initial draft stages. :D

item.title: Re: MOOOOOON GNOOOOOOMESS
item.content:encoded: Thank you for your insight + review! I'd love to do an article for the koboldes and definitely have some ideas.

item.title: MOOOOOON GNOOOOOOMESS
item.content:encoded:
So there is a lot to love about this article. For starters, the descriptions of the gnomes themselves are just adorable. The trade log is fascinating and really helps with world building via the magical folk trade system implied there in. Finally, we are left with the mention of the kolbolde which not only amplifies the world building already done, but leaves me wanting to know even more.

If you haven't considered making an article for the kolboldes, maybe give that a shot as well. I'd be very interested in reading it.

+1. Good job you two.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad you dig it!

re: highfalutin… whoops. Somehow I thought that was German. Corrected!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Makes me wonder what the MGs are doing with the trade goods.

I'll just say that the little hats are for the kids. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dig it. +1

Minor note: I think "highfalutin" in that one translation note isn't quite the word you want (unless linguistics has given a formal meaning to a very informal word.) "Patronizing" or "Pretentious" might be more what you're looking for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this, it's bizarre, and it's well put together. Great work, everyone. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Moon gnomes. I like it, especially the implied friction between the MGs and the kobolden. Makes me wonder what the MGs are doing with the trade goods.

+1, would trade again.

item.title: Re: Intro!
item.content:encoded:
Eyyyyy this was a pretty fun writing adventure. Big thanks to everyone who read this over in chat, especially the people who got excited about MOOOOOON GNOOOOOOMESS. Hooray!

Dr_Grom and Rikjard did an amazing job with the German aspects. Turns out, there are two SCP-DE Foundation sites in Germany within 50 km of the recovery location of this anomaly! Shout-out to the new German branch, they're doing great work.

I had a lot of fun with this. Collabs are fun, everyone!

Edit: If anyone is interested in how we had the collaboration set up with three authors, here's a link to the sandbox we used to keep the discussion and conceptualization together: http://scpsandbox2.wikidot.com/coffeepunk-zyn-collaboration

item.title: Intro!
item.content:encoded:
Hey everyone,
Just a quick bit about this.

Originally I had something pretty different in mind that failed spectacularly but I got talking to Zyn and she offered to co-write this with me. With some help from the good Dr. Grom and Rikjard Regreps, we put together an entirely new iteration of my "moon gnomes" concept. (Sounds funnier than they really are I think).

I love coming up with ideas and concepts but execution is a bit of a weak point with SCPs as I don't have a solid grasp on clinical language. Furthermore, Zyn's background on the site helped tremendously so we could avoid making a clone of something else. It was great going back and forth on stuff.

I'm pretty happy with this and excited about it since I've wanted to get something solid on here for years so I think this is a good (real) attempt at it, so tremendous thank yous to Zyn, Grom, and Rikjard who all played a big part in this. Especially Zyn!

Team:
- Zyn (conceptualization, clinical tone, interview, table content)
- Dr_Grom (conceptualization, German folklore knowledge, German translations, German Branch information)

Contributors:
- Rikjard Regreps (interview translation)

Reviewers:
- (in chat) Conwell, Rimple, SpectralDragon (x2 reads), Tuomey, Lex1nat0r, AbsentmindedNihilist, Snugglewumps, minmin, Vivax
